stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the highway route) yields a guaranteed return of $181K. Option B (the scenic route) depends on traffic conditions: with probability 41-57% (uncertain), it yields $291K; otherwise it yields $63K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic conditions affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $181K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $156.5K to $193.0K. Point estimate: $174.7K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 51.8%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 51.8%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
